lede: A look at August 3 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Zach Wilson and Sven Ulreich, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2023 Slovenia floods
events.desc: Worst floods hit major parts of Slovenia.

events.title: List of heads of state of Mauritania
events.desc: President of Mauritania Maaouya Ould Sid'Ahmed Taya is overthrown in a military coup while attending the funeral of King Fahd in Saudi Arabia.

events.title: Tandy Corporation
events.desc: Tandy Corporation announces the TRS-80, one of the world's first mass-produced personal computers.

events.title: Boeing 707
events.desc: A privately chartered Boeing 707 strikes a mountain peak and crashes near Agadir, Morocco, killing 188.

events.title: Basketball Association of America
events.desc: The Basketball Association of America and the National Basketball League finalize the merger that would create the National Basketball Association.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Italian forces begin the invasion of British Somaliland.

events.title: Internal Macedonian Revolutionary Organization
events.desc: Macedonian rebels in Kruševo proclaim the Kruševo Republic, which exists for only ten days before Ottoman Turks lay waste to the town.

events.title: Firestone (company)
events.desc: The Firestone Tire and Rubber Company is founded.

events.title: Long Turkish War
events.desc: Long War: Austria captures Transylvania in the Battle of Goroszló.

events.title: Pope Stephen IX
events.desc: Frederick of Lorraine elected as Pope Stephen IX.

births.name: Abhisit Vejjajiva
births.line: English-Thai economist and politician, 27th Prime Minister of Thailand

births.name: Roland Burris
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 39th Illinois Attorney General

deaths.text: Edgar Johan Kuusik, Estonian architect and interior designer

observances.text: Christian feast day: George Freeman Bragg, W. E. B. Du Bois (Episcopal Church)
Christian feast day: Olaf II of Norway (Translation of the relic)
Christian feast day: Stephen (Discovery of the relic)
